exit_motivation: Age 63 turning 64, wants to ski more, spend time with 4 grandchildren (5th on the way), hip replacement surgery in May blocking travel until June 8, has been working 12-hour days for years

assets.name: hr.com domain name
assets.description: Premium 2-letter .com domain. Domain brokers estimate $15-20M. SAP, Oracle, Workday, OpenAI, Anthropic, Deloitte, Accenture, PWC all interested. Current domain broker negotiated down from 20% to 10% commission.
assets.notes: Can sell domain separately and rebrand business under HR West, HR Certification, Human Resource Research Institute domains. Already separating content to alternative domains.

assets.name: HR.com media business
assets.description: 27-year profitable B2B digital media/community platform for HR professionals. LinkedIn for HR people. Content from community. Conference business (HR West). Research division. Cert prep business.
assets.notes: Traditional media companies offered 1.2-1.5x revenue multiple. EBITDA ~20%. Revenue used to be larger but last 3 years focused on My People. 2022 process had 8 offers: lowest $9M (industry association), highest $45M. Took one in between that fell through.

assets.name: My People (community platform)
assets.description: Spun-out B2B community platform technology. TypeScript, Node.js, React, API-first, native AI. Rebuilt backend in 2023, went live June 2, 2025. 35 HR tech clients interested, first 7 going live in 45 days. SSO, Salesforce/HubSpot integration, health scores, customer support communities.
assets.notes: Currently funded by HR.com at $250K/year. Needs a CEO (previous partner/co-founder didn't work out). Previous attempt: Forge (F-O-R-J) raised $8M with Baer Baird money, went through 4 CTOs in 3 years, failed. Debbie got IP back in 2023 for $1M. Ready to be funded and spun out once CEO is in place.

action_items.task: Send detailed document outlining HR.com assets and potential acquirers
Provide available times for follow-up call Thursday while traveling
Review asset document and prepare strategic plan with 3 options within 24-48 hours
Collaborate with Ewing to analyze asset document and develop exit strategy and leadership placement plan

key_relationships: Glenn (Daddy Shark) - Ewing's former boss, Debbie's ski buddy and longtime friend. Was her employee 30+ years ago. Lives in Santa Barbara, drives Mercedes Sprinter van. Debbie skiing with him at Mammoth this week.
Bill Philip (Delancey and Partners) - Brought the previous CEO for Forge
Doug Merritt - Mutual contact with John Kelly, Splunk connection
Adam - Former My People co-founder/CEO who didn't work out, had 4-person dev team
Domain broker - Has broker at 10% commission (down from 20%), handles 90% of 2-letter domain sales
deal_notes: Three-asset deal with optionality: can sell bundled or individually. Domain is the crown jewel ($15-20M standalone). Media business is solid but declining from peak. My People is the growth play but needs CEO and funding. Debbie knows all the potential buyers personally — needs us for leverage in negotiations, not introductions. 2022 process produced 8 offers ($9M-$45M range) — high variance suggests packaging matters enormously. She explicitly said doing her own negotiations is 'not the best thing.' Just moved off AWS for data ownership — critical competitive positioning.
competitive_intel: Domain broker already engaged at 10% commission. 2022 process had 8 offers from $9M to $45M. One buyer today showed agentic HR workflow on SAP/Oracle/Workday — only 6 clients, launched January. AI.com domain sold in January as comp. SAP, Workday, Oracle, OpenAI, Anthropic, Deloitte, Accenture, PWC all potential buyers for domain.
owner_personal: Debbie is 63, turning 64 in May. Hip replacement surgery coming — can't travel until June 8. Has 4 grandchildren with 5th on the way. Works 12-hour days. Wants to ski more with Glenn. In Vegas for conference, then Mammoth with Glenn Thurs-Sun. Canadian citizen (was blocked from entering US during COVID). Not miserable — loves to work — but intentionally transitioning. Open to 2-year commitment post-sale. Employees with 20-30 year tenure she wants to protect.
